rawCommits.subject: Fix janky chat scroll at bottom during sidekick resize
rawCommits.body:
Four systems were competing to keep the chat pinned to the bottom while
dragging the sidekick divider: a custom per-message ResizeObserver, a
dedicated streaming-bubble ResizeObserver, a totalSize-keyed layout
effect, and a whole resize-session context plumbed through DesktopShell
into useScrollAnchorV2 plus a settled-effect rAF. The first two fired
scrollTop corrections synchronously inside their observer callbacks
while the virtualizer wrapper still had its pre-commit (stale) totalSize
style, so scrollTop = scrollHeight landed at the old bottom and then
snapped forward when React committed. Reading-mode anchor restoration
used each message's own rect, so it remained visually stable - which is
why only the pinned-at-bottom path looked janky.

Collapse to a single correction path. The virtualizer's own
measureElement drives totalSize; a single useLayoutEffect keyed on
totalSize and the streaming fields runs one post-commit, pre-paint
correction with a fresh scrollHeight. The per-message ResizeObserver is
kept solely to keep the message height cache warm for rows that later
scroll out of the active window - it no longer fires any scroll
correction. Remove the entire ChatResizeSessionContext plumbing,
DesktopShell's onResizeStart wiring, the streaming-bubble observer, and
the settled-effect remeasure pass.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Fix janky chat scroll at bottom during sidekick resize

rawCommits.subject: Pin chat to bottom synchronously during lane resize
rawCommits.body:
The prior refactor consolidated our corrections to a single post-commit
useLayoutEffect on totalSize, but that still leaves a one-frame lag
between the DOM rewrap during a sidekick drag and the React commit that
applies the virtualizer's new totalSize - so when pinned at the bottom,
each drag frame paints with stale translateY values and a stale
scrollHeight, which is the visible "bottom pops around" jank.

TanStack Virtual already has a synchronous hook for exactly this case:
`shouldAdjustScrollPositionOnItemSizeChange`, invoked from inside the
virtualizer's own ResizeObserver callback (pre-paint, same frame as the
layout change). Its default only returns true for items above the
viewport (anchor preservation while reading), which is why the pinned
path looked stable while scrolled up and janky while pinned at bottom.

Hook our `isAutoFollowing` state into it so every item size change
during follow mode accumulates into `scrollAdjustments` and scrollTop
is adjusted by the same delta on the same frame, keeping the visible
bottom pinned. Reading mode keeps the library's default behavior.

ChatMessageList now takes an `isAutoFollowing` prop wired from
ChatPanel (mirrored into a ref so the callback reads the latest
value). The post-commit totalSize/streaming effect is retained as a
safety re-sync but is a no-op in the steady pinned case.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Pin chat to bottom synchronously during lane resize

rawCommits.subject: Stop scrollTop clamp from swallowing pin-to-bottom delta on resize
rawCommits.body:
The previous commit wired `shouldAdjustScrollPositionOnItemSizeChange`
so the virtualizer would try to adjust scrollTop for every visible
item's size change during a sidekick drag. That part works, but the
visible pop was still happening because `resizeItem` calls
`scrollToFn(offset + delta)` *before* `itemSizeCache` is updated and
*well before* React commits the new `totalSize` style on the wrapper
div. So the browser clamped `scrollTop` against the stale
`maxScrollTop = scrollHeight_old - clientHeight`, which swallowed any
adjustment past the last item's delta. The "jump to the real bottom"
then happened on the next frame when React finally committed and our
`useLayoutEffect` wrote `scrollTop = scrollHeight`. With a fixed-height
tool card (spec/file preview) near the tail this was especially
visible: nearly all the delta came from middle prose re-wrapping, the
tail card contributed ~0, so the clamp ate almost the entire
adjustment.

Remove the clamp from the path. Take a ref on the virtualizer wrapper
div (the `position: relative; height: totalSize` one) and, in the
`shouldAdjustScrollPositionOnItemSizeChange` callback, bump
`wrapper.style.height` by the incoming delta and force a layout flush
(`void wrapper.offsetHeight`). That updates `scrollHeight` in the
same frame, so the virtualizer's following `scrollTo` lands at the
true new bottom without being clamped. React's next commit sets
`style.height = totalSize_new`, which matches what we already wrote,
so the reconciler is a no-op. A side benefit: the `scroll` event that
fires when the adjustment actually takes effect now reads a fresh
`scrollHeight` inside our `handleScroll`, so large drags can't flip
`pinnedRef` to false mid-drag on a stale `distFromBottom`.

Only grow, never shrink: when items shrink, the virtualizer's
`scrollTo(target)` target is already below the old max so no clamp
fires; the wrapper naturally reconciles back down on React's next
commit.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Stop scrollTop clamp from swallowing pin-to-bottom delta on resize

rawCommits.subject: Double chat textarea height and move scrollbar to row's right edge
rawCommits.body:
Raise the textarea's max-height from 200px to 400px and relocate the
vertical scrollbar from the textarea itself onto the input row, so it
renders past the send button instead of between the text and the
button. Attach/send buttons use sticky positioning to stay anchored
while the row scrolls.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Double chat textarea height and move scrollbar to row's right edge

rawCommits.subject: Let chat input grow with the viewport
rawCommits.body:
Replace the 400px cap on the chat textarea and its scrolling row with
min(70vh, 800px), and compute the matching JS ceiling from
window.innerHeight * 0.7 (capped at 800). A window-resize listener
re-runs autoResizeTextarea so the height stays correct when the
viewport changes, letting the input use the available lane height
instead of stopping short.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Let chat input grow with the viewport

rawCommits.subject: Unify chat lane artifacts behind a single Block primitive
rawCommits.body:
Every non-prose thing the agent emits (thinking, file ops, commands, specs,
tasks, list results, generated media, status readouts, unknown tools) now
renders through one shared `Block` shell: header with title and badge, a
fixed-height internal-scroll body, and a collapsible chevron. Tool rendering
is dispatched via a small `renderToolBlock` registry, replacing the big
per-tool if/else ladder in `ToolRow`.

Fixed body height is the point: once a Block is mounted it no longer grows
while content streams in, so the chat lane above and below stays stable and
live previews feel like a rolling `tail -f` inside the block instead of a
layout earthquake outside it.

Removed the bespoke `ThinkingRow`, `FilePreviewCard`, `SpecPreviewCard`,
`CommandPreviewCard`, `TaskCreatedIndicator`, and `SuperAgentToolCards`
components in favor of focused renderers under `components/Block/renderers/`.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Unify chat lane artifacts behind a single Block primitive

rawCommits.subject: Match feedback comment input to LLM chat input
rawCommits.body:
Replace the pill-styled comment field with a flush 32px row: send
button moves inside the textarea row, outer padding is gone, and only
a 1px top border separates it from the comment list. The input is now
an auto-resizing textarea and the send icon is ArrowUp, mirroring the
ChatInputBar primitives.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Match feedback comment input to LLM chat input

rawCommits.subject: Add Feedback app (phase 1, mock-backed)
rawCommits.body:
Introduces a new top-level Feedback app that mirrors the Feed/Profile
shell pattern. Left panel has grouped single-select filters for
Trending (sort), Type (category), and Status, each driving a local
Zustand store backed by mock data. Middle panel renders Feed-sized
cards with inline vote controls, a muted header line (author, time,
category), a colored status tag on the right, and a bottom-left
"N comment(s)" button. Right panel matches the Feed comments layout.

Post creation moved to a + button on the sidebar search action that
opens a ZUI Modal styled to match NewProjectModal / AddTaskForm, using
the shared Select component for category/status.

Also fixes a latent bug in resolveActiveApp and mobileNavigation where
pathname.startsWith('/feed') was swallowing '/feedback' into the Feed
app. Both now require a strict basePath match and have regression tests.

The requirements doc is updated to move vote storage to aura-network
(matching the Phase 3 plan) rather than Aura OS local persistence.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Share scroll + folder-section chrome across Feed and Feedback
rawCommits.body:
Extract the Projects left-menu section header into a reusable
FolderSection component (label + rotating chevron + keyboard toggle +
optional suffix), and use it for the three Feedback left-panel groups
(Trending, Type, Status) in place of ad-hoc headings.

Also switch Feed and Feedback to the shared OverlayScrollbar used by
LeftMenuTree / ChatPanel / SidekickContent: main list, comments list,
and the Feedback filter panel now hide their native scrollbars and
render the overlay track as a sibling inside a position:relative shell.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Share scroll + folder-section chrome across Feed and Feedback

rawCommits.subject: Replace chat virtualization with native CSS scroll anchoring
rawCommits.body:
The virtualizer rendered each message with `position: absolute;
transform: translateY(start)` where `start` came from a cumulative sum
in the virtualizer's measurementsCache. React committed those translateY
values one frame after the ResizeObserver that caused them, so every
drag frame painted with stale positions before correcting, which the
user experienced as items sliding and popping during a sidekick lane
resize.

The last four commits tried to fix this from the JS side - running our
own anchor restoration via rAF-coalesced scroll writes, then forcing
the virtualizer's `shouldAdjustScrollPositionOnItemSizeChange` to true,
then imperatively bumping the wrapper's `style.height` to defeat
`scrollTop` clamping against a stale `scrollHeight`. Each step was
correct in isolation but none addressed the root: virtualization's
async-commit model is fundamentally at odds with synchronous scroll
adjustment inside a ResizeObserver batch, and the more JS we added
the more we fought the browser's own `overflow-anchor` mechanism,
which was being explicitly disabled in CSS *and* rendered moot by the
items' absolute positioning (abs-positioned elements are excluded from
scroll-anchor candidates by spec).

Switch to normal flex-column flow and let CSS scroll anchoring keep
the visible position stable. The browser compensates `scrollTop` for
any height change above the in-viewport anchor element within the same
layout pass, covering lane drags, streaming tokens that land in
messages above the viewport, tool-row expand/collapse, and
load-older-history prepends. The only case native anchoring doesn't
cover is growth *at* the anchor itself - the streaming bubble gaining
tokens, or a new message arriving while pinned - and for that we keep
a targeted `useLayoutEffect` that writes `scrollTop = scrollHeight`,
gated on `isAutoFollowing`.

Files:
- ChatMessageList: drop useVirtualizer + per-message ResizeObserver +
  measureElement plumbing + the wrapper-height hack + the
  totalSize-keyed pin effect. Render messages in a flex column; one
  small useLayoutEffect pins to bottom when the tail grows.
- ChatPanel.module.css: replace the two `overflow-anchor: none` rules
  on `.messageArea*` with `overflow-anchor: auto` so the browser can
  actually anchor.
- useScrollAnchorV2: drop captureAnchor, restoreAnchor,
  reconcileContent, onContentHeightChange, scrollToBottomIfPinned, the
  resize-session branches, and findFirstVisibleMessage. What's left is
  just handleScroll / scrollToBottom / isAutoFollowing plus the guard
  that keeps our own programmatic scroll writes from flipping the
  pinned state.
- useChatPanelState + ChatPanel.tsx: stop plumbing heightCache,
  captureAnchor, restoreAnchor, onContentHeightChange down into
  ChatMessageList.
- useLoadOlderMessages: drop captureAnchor / restoreAnchor arguments -
  the browser preserves the reading position automatically when
  content is prepended above the viewport.
- useMessageHeightCache: delete. No longer needed; the virtualizer was
  the only consumer.

Net -533 lines across 11 files. Tests updated to match the new surface;
33/33 chat tests pass. Lint count is below the pre-change baseline
because the old `any`-cast test fixtures were replaced with a typed
helper.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Replace chat virtualization with native CSS scroll anchoring

rawCommits.subject: Neutralize Block body borders and highlight colors
rawCommits.body:
Two chat-lane polish fixes, both scoped to the Block primitive so every
renderer inherits them automatically:

- Strip the outer-markdown pre border/padding that ancestor rulesets
  (`.root pre` in LLMOutput.module.css) were painting onto renderer
  content nested inside a Block body. Every Block already owns its shell
  border, so the SpecBlock no longer shows a second inner border.
- Collapse highlight.js colors inside a Block body onto the app's gray/
  white ramp: structural tokens (section headings, keywords, tags, bullets,
  strong/emphasis) use --color-text, everything else uses
  --color-text-secondary, comments use --color-text-muted. Spec and file
  previews now read in the same palette as the rest of the UI instead of
  the imported github-dark rainbow.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Neutralize Block body borders and highlight colors

rawCommits.subject: Align Block body text with header status dot
rawCommits.body:
Code, spec, and diff renderers use `flushBody` on the Block (the body
wrapper has no padding) so content can sit edge-to-edge with the scroll
viewport. That meant the previous 0-padding reset on the inner `pre` let
content slam against the left border instead of lining up with the
header's green status dot (10px from the block edge).

Move the 10px horizontal offset onto the `.codeArea` / `.diffArea`
wrappers themselves so code text, spec markdown, and diff lines all start
at the same x as the status dot and the title icon above them.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Align Block body text with header status dot

rawCommits.subject: Wire up feedback API end-to-end
rawCommits.body:
Backend:
- New `handlers/feedback.rs` with list / create / get / update-status /
  list-comments / add-comment / cast-vote endpoints, plus a router
  group in `router.rs` and the module entry in `handlers/mod.rs`.
- Matching integration-test file `tests/feedback_api_tests.rs`.
- Incidental rustfmt cleanups in `agents/chat.rs`, `spec_disk.rs`,
  `specs.rs`, `tests/api_tests.rs` (no behavior changes).

Frontend:
- New `interface/src/api/feedback.ts` client, surfaced via the root
  `api` object in `api/client.ts`.
- Feedback store rewritten to drive through the real API; mock-data
  module and its imports removed.
- `FeedbackMainPanel` and `NewFeedbackModal` adjusted to the
  API-backed store; store tests updated to match.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Add session delete and restyle sidekick sessions as task trees
rawCommits.body:
Right-click a session row in the Project sidekick Sessions tab or the
Agents app Chats tab to delete it. Sessions now render as an Explorer
tree with TaskStatusIcon suffixes so they visually match specs/tasks.

Backend wires a new DELETE /api/projects/:p/agents/:a/sessions/:s route
through to StorageClient.delete_session, with a mock-storage handler so
the integration test can exercise the full proxy path.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Add session delete and restyle sidekick sessions as task trees

rawCommits.subject: Scope session reset to drop prior-session LLM context
rawCommits.body:
Reset only cleared the in-memory caches and rotated the storage write
target — the cold-start rebuild still aggregated every past session, so a
corrupted tool_use block from a crashed harness kept getting re-injected
into the model on any cache miss / cold start / refresh. Split the read
path: UI history keeps aggregating across sessions while LLM-context
loaders now scope to the current storage session only. Also strip
dangling tool_use blocks (no matching tool_result) in both the
super-agent and harness conversion paths as a defensive guard against
future harness crashes.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Scope session reset to drop prior-session LLM context

rawCommits.subject: Collapse doubled borders between stacked tool call rows
rawCommits.body:
Adjacent Block borders in the activity timeline's tool group sat flush
with zero gap, so a row's top border stacked against the previous row's
bottom border and read as a 2px divider. Pull each subsequent row up by
-1px so neighboring borders overlap into a single clean 1px line.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Collapse doubled borders between stacked tool call rows

rawCommits.subject: Wire feedback votes and status updates through aura-network
rawCommits.body:
Replaces the phase-2 501 stubs: list/get now surface vote aggregates
inline, PATCH status forwards to aura-network's post metadata endpoint,
and POST vote round-trips through the new votes API. The UI applies
votes/status optimistically and reconciles with server aggregates,
reverting on failure.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Wire feedback votes and status updates through aura-network

rendered.title: Chat Performance & Feedback System Launch
rendered.intro: Major chat scrolling improvements and the launch of a new Feedback app with full backend integration. Chat now uses native CSS scroll anchoring for smooth resizing, while the new Feedback system provides voting, commenting, and status tracking.

entries.title: Chat Performance Overhaul
entries.summary: Eliminated janky scrolling during sidekick resizing and unified tool rendering with a new Block primitive for stable layouts.

items.text: Fixed chat scroll jank during sidekick resize by consolidating four competing scroll correction systems into a single post-commit layout effect
Unified all chat tool artifacts (thinking, file ops, commands, specs) behind a single Block primitive with fixed-height scrollable bodies to prevent layout shifts during streaming
Enhanced chat input with 70vh height limit, viewport-responsive sizing, and relocated scrollbar to the row's right edge
Launched new Feedback app with voting, commenting, and status management, including full API backend and mock data integration

entries.title: Chat Architecture Simplification
entries.summary: Replaced complex virtualization with native CSS scroll anchoring and integrated feedback system with real API endpoints.

items.text: Replaced chat virtualization with native CSS scroll anchoring, eliminating 533 lines of complex scroll management code while achieving smoother performance
Added shared OverlayScrollbar component and FolderSection primitive used across Feed and Feedback apps for consistent UI patterns
Implemented complete feedback API backend with list, create, get, update-status, comments, and voting endpoints plus integration tests
Added session deletion with right-click context menu and restyled session lists as Explorer trees with status icons
Scoped session reset to drop prior-session LLM context, preventing corrupted tool blocks from previous sessions affecting new conversations

entries.title: Feedback System Integration
entries.summary: Connected feedback voting and status updates to aura-network with optimistic UI updates and server reconciliation.

items.text: Wired feedback votes and status updates through aura-network API with optimistic UI updates that reconcile with server aggregates and revert on failure

highlights: Chat scrolling performance dramatically improved with native CSS scroll anchoring
New Feedback app launched with full voting and commenting system
Unified Block primitive stabilizes chat tool rendering during streaming
Session management enhanced with deletion and improved visual hierarchy
